post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 158: Chocolate Factory Mafia
Credits to summitwei and whoever he credited for the rule template.

Please read all the rules, especially if this is your first game.
Do not post if you are not in this game.
Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me(or the comod) at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me or the comod, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator or the comod, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.


Voting/Lynches
  • This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this; see below rule.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • To show that you have read and understand the rules, find any rules that pertain to fake votes, and send them to Nike100/Charge in your role PM.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • At a Lynch-or-Lose situation, which is three town and two mafia, or two town and one mafia currently alive, votes are locked. This means that any attempt to unvote will be ignored, regardless of what the vote is.


Activity
  • Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (Nike100) am the Moderator of Game 158. Charge is the co-moderator of Game 158.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. The comod has second to last say, aka last say at that moment that they say, unless I have specified otherwise.
  • To confirm that you have read the rules till the end please post the word "Confirm" in bold in your first few posts.


Setup
This game will use the Backup6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

Note that if applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action. The power of the Universal Backup cannot be roleblocked.

A Town Doctor is not able to self-protect.


Role PMs
Factory Worker (Vanilla Townie) wrote:
Hello xxx:

You are a Factory Worker (Vanilla Townie).
You hope to protect the legacy of Willy Wonka by rooting out the factory spies.

Win Condition: All spies (mafia) dead.
Your powers lie in your voice and vote.
Grandpa Joe (Town Jailkeeper) wrote:
Hello xxx:

You are Grandpa Joe (Town Jailkeeper).
You hope to protect Willy Wonka's secret recipes. Although you are very powerful, you dislike any violence.

Win condition: All mafia dead
Your power lies in your voice and vote, and in addition your control of the Chocolate Room. Each night you may PM Nike one person, whom you will trap in the Chocolate room with sticky Swudge. They will be unable to get out of the room, much less do anything effective. In addition, because the room is locked with your special keys, nobody else will be able to enter the room.
Charlie Bucket (Town Cop) wrote:
Hello xxx:

You are Charlie Bucket (Town Cop), the new owner of the factory.
You hope to protect the factory's secret recipes and find out who the spies are.

Win Condition: All mafia dead
Your power lies in your voice and vote, and in addition your newly developed hypnotic candy. Whoever ingests such a candy each night, determined by your PM to Nike, will answer one yes or no question you ask truthfully. Hopefully you'll use that question to determine their alliance, and not whether they like you or not.
Grandpa Joe (Town Doctor) wrote:
Hello xxx:

You are Grandpa Joe (Town Doctor).
You hope to protect Willy Wonka's secret recipes. Although you are very powerful, you dislike any violence.

Win Condition: All mafia dead
Your power lies in your voice and vote, and in addition, your possession of the Fizzy Lifting Drinks. Each night you may PM Nike one person, whom you will give the soda to. They will be able to float that night, and because the spies do not have access to this soda, they will be unable to do anything to that person. You cannot drink the soda, however, because you cannot burp.
Charlie Bucket (Universal Backup) wrote:
Hello xxx:

You are Charlie (Universal Backup).
You hope to protect the factory's secret recipe and will inherit the factory when you are old enough, or when your guardian, Grandpa Joe, dies.

Win Condition: All mafia dead
Your power lies in your voice and vote, and in addition, your status as the co-owner of the factory. Once your grandfather dies, you will inherit all all of his possessions and powers.
Charlie Bucket/Grandpa Joe (Town Tracker) wrote:
Hello xxx:

You are the new Oompa Loompa Chief (Town Tracker).
You hope to protect the factory's secret recipe...

Win Condition: All mafia dead
Your power lies in your voice and vote. In addition, because of your newly developed Stalking candy, your stealth, and your smaller than normal stature, you may find out where a person, whose name you will PM Nike, went each night.
Veruca Salt/Augustus Gloop/Mike Teevee (Mafia Goon) wrote:
You are Veruca Salt/Augustus Gloop/Mike Teevee (Mafia Goon).
Your goal is to find the top secret recipes of the chocolate factory and because you are paranoid, kill all other factory workers. Every night, you and your partner, xxx, are allowed communication in [quicktopic link].

Win Condition: Number of mafia is greater than or equal to number of town.
Your power lies in your deceit in the day and your attainment of the Vita-Wonk, a powerful creation of Willy Wonka that can age a victim to death. Each night you and your partner may target one person with the Vita-Wonk, killing them.
Violet Beauregarde (Mafia Roleblocker) wrote:
Hello xxx:

You are Violet Beauregarde (Mafia Roleblocker).
Your goal is to find the top secret recipes of the chocolate factory and because you are paranoid, kill all other factory workers. Every night, you and your partner, xxx are allowed communication in [quicktopic link].

Wind Condition: Number of mafia is greater than or equal to number of town.
Your power lies in your acting skills during the day and your collaborated assassination with your partner every night. Also, because you have bought bubblegum from the thief Prodnose on the black market, you can now use it to trap any one person, whose name you shall PM Nike, and that person will be unable to perform any action because they will be trapped in the bubble.


Alive (5)
  1. fdas++
  2. summitwei++
  3. aragornmf mathjay
  4. etr217
  5. jkittykitkat Kagebaka


Dead (4)
Nike100 (Willy Wonka) has ran out of time.
Charge (Chief Oompa Loompa) has died from a shot to the head.
BZ1, Augustus Gloop (Mafia Goon), was lynched Day 1.
math_turtle37, a Factory Worker (Vanilla Townie) died Night 1.
SFGiantsFan, a Factory Worker (Vanilla Townie), was lynched Day 2.
mark888, Grandpa Joe the Jailkeeper died Night 1.


Significant Events
*mark888 replaces Justano
*math_turtle37 replaces PokeMathist
*Kagebaka replaces jkittykitkat
BZ1, Augustus Gloop (Mafia Goon), was lynched Day 1.
math_turtle37, a Factory Worker (Vanilla Townie) died Night 1.
SFGiantsFan, a Factory Worker (Vanilla Townie), was lynched Day 2.
mark888, Grandpa Joe the Jailkeeper died Night 1.


Warnings
etr217 has received a double warning for OOT.
BZ1 has received a warning for editing post #383
BZ1 has received a warning for editing post #457


Do not post until this line is crossed out.
You may now post.

**

post_rendered:
It seemed as if Charlie would smoothly inherit the chocolate factory from Willy Wonka...
Until one day...Willy Wonka recieved an assassination threat: a man clothed in black with a Guy Fawkes mask suddenly appeared in front of him. That night, Willy Wonka died, aged to death it seemed by his own creation, the Vita-Wonk. It appeared that he did not tell the man what he wanted. The next morning, Chief Oompa Loompa, who was the first to discover Wonka's body, saw a message written in blood: "Give me the Wonka-Vite or you shall die." It was a spy: only trusted factory workers knew of the Wonka-Vite candy's existence. The next morning, Chief Oompa Loompa went to broadcast the news to the whole factory. She turned on the microphone in the room, and said "Spies have infiltrated into the factory! Find out who they are before they steal the-" when a shot rang out in the room. [i]It seemed as if Charlie would smoothly inherit the chocolate factory from Willy Wonka...[/i]
Until one day...Willy Wonka recieved an assassination threat: a man clothed in black with a Guy Fawkes mask suddenly appeared in front of him. That night, Willy Wonka died, aged to death it seemed by his own creation, the Vita-Wonk. It appeared that he did not tell the man what he wanted. The next morning, Chief Oompa Loompa, who was the first to discover Wonka's body, saw a message written in blood: "Give me the Wonka-Vite or you shall die." It was a spy: only trusted factory workers knew of the Wonka-Vite candy's existence. The next morning, Chief Oompa Loompa went to broadcast the news to the whole factory. She turned on the microphone in the room, and said "Spies have infiltrated into the factory! Find out who they are before they steal the-" when a shot rang out in the room. Chief Oompa Loompa fell down dead.

The workers were stunned. With the chaos bound to ensue from the death of both Willy Wonka and the Chief Oompa Loompa, how were they to find the number of spies, much less who they were?

jkittykitkat wrote:
OMGUS means You suck! (when you vote someone because they voted for you, but in a friendly way)
RVS means Random Voting Session (where people vote to get reactions and are not serious)
So this is mostly accurate, but I wanna go into a little more depth. I keep forgetting to update the FAQ with this stuff.

OMGUS can be done later in the game, and what it really means is "You're voting me, and if you were town you wouldn't be voting me because you'd know better, so you're scum, so I'm voting you back." Early game it's mostly a joke.
RVS is a phase we do early in the game to try to get the action going artificially. You're meant to find something alignment indicative out of RVS, so that we can eventually move towards actual gameplay. One way people do this is to attempt to weasel as much as possible out of posts.
For example:
fdas wrote:
jkittykitkat wrote:
I'm surprised that no one said it but... Great Flavor! (Looks for the upvo... Oh.)

So Unvote Vote: fdas cause he lurked (a tiny bit) and he's an IC. Please unvote me.

I've been found!

Vote: fdas
This casual manner reads town to me.

Of course, this kind of thought process isn't the most reliable, but it is helpful for bringing the game to a point where we can actually get things out of posts.

jkittykitkat wrote:
OMGUS means You suck! (when you vote someone because they voted for you, but in a friendly way)
RVS means Random Voting Session (where people vote to get reactions and are not serious)
So this is mostly accurate, but I wanna go into a little more depth. I keep forgetting to update the FAQ with this stuff.

OMGUS can be done later in the game, and what it really means is "You're voting me, and if you were town you wouldn't be voting me because you'd know better, so you're scum, so I'm voting you back." Early game it's mostly a joke.
RVS is a phase we do early in the game to try to get the action going artificially. You're meant to find something alignment indicative out of RVS, so that we can eventually move towards actual gameplay. One way people do this is to attempt to weasel as much as possible out of posts.
For example:
fdas wrote:
jkittykitkat wrote:
I'm surprised that no one said it but... Great Flavor! (Looks for the upvo... Oh.)
Hey SFGiantsFan and summitwei! I'm your biggest fan! I'm a bit wary of the SE/ICs cause one is prob a maf.

From the Confirms, aragornmf and PokeMathist seems town. Everyone else needs to post more.

@above That's OMGUS, and this is RVS (read the FAQ)
So Unvote Vote: fdas cause he lurked (a tiny bit) and he's an IC. Please unvote me.

I've been found!

Vote: fdas
This casual manner reads town to me.

Of course, this kind of thought process isn't the most reliable, but it is helpful for bringing the game to a point where we can actually get things out of posts.

Trying to move along RVS. Also, more IC/SE help. Not much else here than that in this post.

Nike100 wrote:
Welcome to Mafia Game 158: Chocolate Factory Mafia
Credits to summitwei and whoever he credited for the rule template.

Please read all the rules, especially if this is your first game.
Do not post if you are not in this game.
Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me(or the comod) at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me or the comod, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator or the comod, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.


Voting/Lynches
  • This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this; see below rule.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • To show that you have read and understand the rules, find any rules that pertain to fake votes, and send them to Nike100/Charge in your role PM.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • At a Lynch-or-Lose situation, which is three town and two mafia, or two town and one mafia currently alive, votes are locked. This means that any attempt to unvote will be ignored, regardless of what the vote is.


Activity
  • Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (Nike100) am the Moderator of Game 158. Charge is the co-moderator of Game 158.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. The comod has second to last say, aka last say at that moment that they say, unless I have specified otherwise.
  • To confirm that you have read the rules till the end please post the word "Confirm" in bold in your first few posts.


Setup
This game will use the Backup6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker

[size=150]Please read all the rules, especially if this is your first game.
Do not post if you are not in this game.[/size]
[hide="Rules"]
[list]
[*]Read the rules. Including this one.
[*]Follow all the rules. Including this one.
[*]Do not post at night.
[*]You may PM me(or the comod) at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me or the comod, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
[*]Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator or the comod, unless I specifically allow you to do so. 
[*]You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
[*]Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
 [*]If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
[*]Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.
[/list][/hide]

[hide="Voting/Lynches"]
[list]
[*][b]This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.[/b]
[*]Post votes in bold font in the format "[b]Vote: <insert player here>[/b]". For example, "[b]Vote: fdas[/b]" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: [b]fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote fdas[/b]", "[b]vote: fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote:fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote: fads[/b]", "[b]Vote: fdsa[/b]", "[b]Vote: Fdas[/b]" and "[u]Vote: fdas[/u]" are not.
[*]Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
[*]Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. [b]There is one exception to this; see below rule.[/b]
[*]The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. 
[*]You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "[b]Vote: No Lynch[/b]".
[*]You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "[b]Unvote[/b]". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
[*]Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
[*]Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
[*]The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
[*]To show that you have read and understand the rules, find any rules that pertain to fake votes, and send them to Nike100/Charge in your role PM.
[*]During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
[*]Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
[*]At a Lynch-or-Lose situation, which is three town and two mafia, or two town and one mafia currently alive, [b]votes are locked[/b]. This means that any attempt to unvote will be ignored, regardless of what the vote is.[/list]
[/hide]

[hide="Activity"]
[list]
[*]Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
[*]The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
[*]Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
[*]When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
[*]Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
[*]If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
[*]If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
[*]If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
[*]If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.
[/list]
[/hide]

[hide="Etiquette"]
[list]
[*]Do not use inappropriate language.
[*]Do not make any personal attacks.
[*]Be mature.
[*]For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.[/list]
[/hide]

[hide="Moderators"]
[list]
[*]I (Nike100) am the Moderator of Game 158. Charge is the co-moderator of Game 158.
[*]Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
[*]Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
[*]Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
[*]Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
[*]The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. The comod has second to last say, aka last say at that moment that they say, unless I have specified otherwise.
[*]To confirm that you have read the rules till the end please post the word "Confirm" in bold in your first few posts.[/list]
[/hide]

post_rendered:
So etr I love the work you're putting into this game but you're making a few mistakes that merely need experience to fix but I'm gonna try to cheat a bit and get them fixed before

There are a lot of things that seem like things that scum would do but town wouldn't - for example scumclaiming (what town would scumclaim?)

But it works the other way around - what scum would scumclaim? When this kind of thing happens, it's very likely going to just be something the person would be doing regardless of alignment and is therefore NAI.


The way that I like to find tells that are (hopefully) alignment indicative is to look at motivations; for example, why would a player make a post as either alignment? Does this make them more likely to be town or scum? If they are scum, does this imply anything about anybody else? This is not 100% consistent, because sometimes you can get their motivations wrong, but with practice this can be a very effective way to scumhunt, or at least to get more information.

jkittykitkat wrote:
OMGUS means You suck! (when you vote someone because they voted for you, but in a friendly way)
RVS means Random Voting Session (where people vote to get reactions and are not serious)
So this is mostly accurate, but I wanna go into a little more depth. I keep forgetting to update the FAQ with this stuff.

OMGUS can be done later in the game, and what it really means is "You're voting me, and if you were town you wouldn't be voting me because you'd know better, so you're scum, so I'm voting you back." Early game it's mostly a joke.
RVS is a phase we do early in the game to try to get the action going artificially. You're meant to find something alignment indicative out of RVS, so that we can eventually move towards actual gameplay. One way people do this is to attempt to weasel as much as possible out of posts.
For example:

jkittykitkat wrote:
OMGUS means You suck! (when you vote someone because they voted for you, but in a friendly way)
RVS means Random Voting Session (where people vote to get reactions and are not serious)
So this is mostly accurate, but I wanna go into a little more depth. I keep forgetting to update the FAQ with this stuff.

OMGUS can be done later in the game, and what it really means is "You're voting me, and if you were town you wouldn't be voting me because you'd know better, so you're scum, so I'm voting you back." Early game it's mostly a joke.
RVS is a phase we do early in the game to try to get the action going artificially. You're meant to find something alignment indicative out of RVS, so that we can eventually move towards actual gameplay. One way people do this is to attempt to weasel as much as possible out of posts.

Story
The workers had been busy producing more candy than ever, but they always took time to attend discussion sessions about who the spies were. PokeMathist, one of the factory workers, insulted many of the workers with his claim that they were wasting their time on conspiracy theories, which were of no use at all. A few of the senior workers found PokeMathist suspicious for this, accusing that he didn't want to root out the spies, but the majority consensus was that PokeMathist was innocent. After this quarrel was settled, the workers had a moment of realization. Rather than standing out by accusing those participating in "conspiracy theories", a spy would blend in by working all day- and that would also be an excuse to avoid discussion. Thus everyone's attention was turned onto those who didn't attend any of the discussion sessions. They decided that BZ1, a worker who seemed too nonchalant about the assassination of Willy Wonka and Chief Oompa Loompa, was a culprit. BZ1, Augustus Gloop (Mafia Goon), was lynched Day 1.

The factory workers cheered; they had found a spy! But of course, there was/were still at least 1 alive...


Day has ended; night has begun. Night will end in 3 days, or on 6:05 PM (PST) on February 21, 2018.

[hide="Story"]The workers had been busy producing more candy than ever, but they always took time to attend discussion sessions about who the spies were. PokeMathist, one of the factory workers, insulted many of the workers with his claim that they were wasting their time on conspiracy theories, which were of no use at all. A few of the senior workers found PokeMathist suspicious for this, accusing that he didn't want to root out the spies, but the majority consensus was that PokeMathist was innocent. After this quarrel was settled, the workers had a moment of realization. Rather than standing out by accusing those participating in "conspiracy theories", a spy would blend in by working all day- and that would also be an excuse to avoid discussion. Thus everyone's attention was turned onto those who didn't attend any of the discussion sessions. They decided that BZ1, a worker who seemed too nonchalant about the assassination of Willy Wonka and Chief Oompa Loompa, was a culprit. [color=#f00]BZ1, Augustus Gloop (Mafia Goon)[/color], was lynched Day 1.

The factory workers cheered; they had found a spy! But of course, there was/were still at least 1 alive...[/hide]

[hide=proof:]
assume $a=b$
$ab=b^2$
$ab-a^2=b^2-a^2$
factor the left and use product of squares on the right:

$a=b+a$
since $a=b$, we substitute a for b and get
$a=2a$
$1=2$
$0=1$

Even if you think your posts haven't been that towny, you know you're town, so you should treat yourself like town.

Your SFGF read is great in this regard, but your scumread on etr just seems to be "everybody else thinks he's scum so I guess he must be scummy". fdas wrote:
1) meta
meta should never be used to show someone is towny. And since you are the one bringing it up to show that you yourself are town, it becomes even more useless than it already is.
Meta can actually be used to show someone is towny if it is used correctly (which it rarely ever is). The important distinction is that you are looking for thought processes, not for specific actions. One thing that is unlikely to change between games without a legitimate reason for it is philosophy about the game, and this can be abused.


I'll use myself here as an example because I can. Multiple times, I have stated my philosophy on bussing - it should never be done unless you are trying to stop the game from being solved. I have been shown to put this philosophy into practice in games such as 149, so it is not just something I say, and have constantly argued for it, so I do believe in it.

So voting BZ1 and pushing for his lynch, in this case, makes no sense. He should not have seemed like a "lost cause" from my perspective - one night is more than enough to set any scum on the right track. He had no w/w interactions with me, and did not seem particularly likely to create any more in the future. I am aware my first vote on him was for motivation, but I had multiple times where I could have easily gotten out of the vote. There was, overall, no reason for me to stay on the BZ1 wagon if I did not wish to. My philosophy on bussing, quite frankly, makes this vote not worth it.

fdas wrote:
2) motivation
In what way? You not being on the SFGF wagon yesterday shows nothing. You did not need to vote him to get him lynched. Actually, if you were mafia now are getting the best of both worlds by having SFGF dead and by coming out of the whole thing looking towny.
Please. I'm obviously not referring to the SFGF wagon - that horrible wagon should have never happened; and someone like me being on it would have been a scumclaim. One of the things I am referring to the most is my view on mark. It was very obvious to me that mark was a PR from D1 (this was my "meta" townread on him; fdas, I assume you had a similar reason). So there is absolutely no reason for scum me to have NK'ed math_turtle. This is, of course, an argument to which many people will reply, "But summit! What about the WIFOM? Couldn't you have made this exact argument, and gotten yourself towncleared for no reason?" Nope. Cases in which WIFOM apply are logistic rather than mechanical; no amount of WIFOM will save you from a cop guilty. With this in mind, scum me never ever kills someone like math_turtle. You cannot find a legitimate scum motivation for what I gain from killing math_turtle; however, for multiple other people, he could have easily been the best kill, or at least one that's worth it (I won't go into reasons, though I can think of several).
I can also throw on my thought process to vote for etr instead of mathjay D2, but that's a little harder to explain and I'm lazy.

I do believe this is a weaker argument than 1) meta, because of the fact that gambits exist. The very fact that a gambit is necessitated to justify such a play, however, lowers its chance significantly.

Meta can actually be used to show someone is towny if it is used correctly (which it rarely ever is). The important distinction is that you are looking for [i]thought processes[/i], not for [i]specific actions[/i]. One thing that is unlikely to change between games without a legitimate reason for it is philosophy about the game, and this can be abused.


I'll use myself here as an example because I can. Multiple times, I have stated my philosophy on bussing - it should never be done unless you are trying to stop the game from being solved. I have been shown to put this philosophy into practice in games such as 149, so it is not just something I say, and have constantly argued for it, so I do believe in it.

So voting BZ1 and pushing for his lynch, in this case, makes no sense. He should not have seemed like a "lost cause" from my perspective - one night is more than enough to set any scum on the right track. He had no w/w interactions with me, and did not seem particularly likely to create any more in the future. I am aware my first vote on him was for motivation, but I had multiple times where I could have easily gotten out of the vote. There was, overall, [b]no reason[/b] for me to stay on the BZ1 wagon if I did not wish to. My philosophy on bussing, quite frankly, makes this vote not worth it.
